subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle v(t) = cos(πt/6) — Moving Left, Distance Integral, Acceleration/Speed & Position at t=4
stimulus: For $0 \le t \le 12$, a particle moves along the $x$-axis. The velocity of the particle at time $t$ is given by $v(t) = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right)$. The particle is at position $x = -2$ at time $t = 0$.

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 12$, when is the particle moving to the left?
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Finds $v(t) = 0$: $\cos(\frac{\pi t}{6}) = 0 \Rightarrow t = 3$ and $t = 9$ on $[0, 12]$
1 pt: Correct interval: the particle moves to the left when $v(t) < 0$, which is $3 < t < 9$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(t) = 0$ at $t = 3$ and $t = 9$. $v(t) < 0$ for $3 < t < 9$. Particle moves left on $(3, 9)$.

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the total distance traveled by the particle from time $t = 0$ to time $t = 6$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct expression: $\int_0^6 |v(t)|\, dt = \int_0^6 \left|\cos\left(\frac{\pi t}{6}\right)\right|\, dt$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Total distance $= \int_0^6 |v(t)|\, dt$ (do not evaluate).

parts.prompt: Find the acceleration of the particle at time $t$. Is the speed of the particle increasing, decreasing, or neither at time $t = 4$? Explain your reasoning.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct conclusion: speed is increasing at $t = 4$ because velocity and acceleration have the same sign (both negative)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $a(t) = -\frac{\pi}{6}\sin(\frac{\pi t}{6})$. At $t=4$: $v(4) = -\frac{1}{2}$ and $a(4) = -\frac{\sqrt{3}\pi}{12}$; both negative (same sign), so speed increasing.

parts.prompt: Find the position of the particle at time $t = 4$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up integral: $x(4) = -2 + \int_0^4 \cos\left(\frac{\pi t}{6}\right)\, dt$
